spec_match.rationale: The concept now reads selection from the editor's own mouseup and click events, then opens an inline toolbar from that immediate click selection instead of testing unrelated toolbar-button clicks.

interactive.rationale: Users drag-select textarea text, see mouseup/click/deferred readouts, and apply inline wrapping actions from the toolbar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature index and page both describe a textarea inline-selection toolbar that exercises the Chrome 150 ordering fix.

edge_cases.rationale: The page labels no-selection state, compares direct and deferred reads before mutation, and uses a cached editor selection for toolbar actions.

references.rationale: Includes the required ChromeStatus reference link.

design_system.rationale: Uses shared showcase tokens and avoids raw color literals.

summary: The flawed rich-text toolbar comparison was replaced with an editor-triggered inline selection toolbar, the index blurb was aligned, and the missing ChromeStatus link was added.
